concerns.text: FR-007 mandates 'family-wise error correction (e.g., Bonferroni or False Discovery Rate)' for predictor importance. The idea and methodology sketch only require 'permutation importance analysis' to rank predictors. Permutation importance is a model-agnostic metric of feature contribution, not a statistical hypothesis test requiring p-values or multiple-comparison correction. This requirement adds a statistical layer not requested by the idea and potentially misapplied to the chosen method.

SC-005 requires a 'sensitivity analysis of the classification threshold' sweeping {0.3, 0.5, 0.7}. The idea's 'Expected results' and 'Methodology sketch' focus on ROC-AUC (which is threshold-independent) and identifying top predictors. While threshold analysis is useful, explicitly mandating a specific sweep range and reporting false-positive/negative rates for these specific cutoffs constitutes scope expansion beyond the core research question of 'accurately predict... susceptibility' via model performance and driver identification.

The 'Threshold Justification' assumption states the 0.5 threshold is 'justified as the standard... but a sensitivity analysis (FR-007) is required'. This references FR-007 (Bonferroni correction) instead of the actual threshold analysis requirement (which appears to be SC-005 or an unnumbered requirement). This internal inconsistency suggests the spec requirements are not fully aligned with the assumptions or each other.
